summary: Dedup the mkdir-parent-then-write-file mechanic duplicated 4x across bundle.rs, install.rs, and drift.rs (drift.rs carries two copies itself)

new.description: Leaf module (flat, alongside path.rs's precedent for a small shared no-error-type-coupling mechanic): `pub(crate) fn write_creating_parents(path: &Path, bytes: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<()>` â€” create the path's parent dirs then write the bytes, the one implementation of the mechanic every call site below currently reimplements.

edit.description: Declare `pub(crate) mod fs_util;` alongside the other leaf modules (path.rs at line 55).

`write_text` (342-361) inlines create_dir_all+fs::write mapped to BundleError::Write; collapse its body to one call to `fs_util::write_creating_parents`, mapping the io::Error into BundleError::Write â€” call sites (write_member, write_json) unchanged.
`write_scaffold_file` (1454-1462) is the identical mechanic mapped to InstallError::Write; collapse its body to one call to `fs_util::write_creating_parents`.

Two internal copies of the same mechanic, both mapped to DriftError::Write: `create_dir_all`/`write_bytes` (517-528, used by write_rollup) and `write_placement`'s inline mkdir+write (2543-2553, used by the merge/apply path). Collapse both to `fs_util::write_creating_parents`; write_rollup's create_dir_all(into) + write_bytes(...) becomes one call, write_placement's inline block becomes one call â€” no signature or call-site change at either site.

acceptance: `rg -B2 'fs::write\(' src/*.rs` shows the create-parent-dirs-then-write mechanic implemented exactly once, in src/fs_util.rs; bundle.rs, install.rs, and drift.rs's two former call sites all delegate to it with behavior unchanged (existing tests/bundle.rs, tests/install.rs, and drift's lock/placement round-trip tests stay green with no edits).
notes: Found this tick's posture sweep of src/bundle.rs's neighborhood (install.rs and drift.rs are its immediate imports via install::session_start_group/SESSION_START_COMMAND and drift::project_bytes). Pure mechanical dedup, no behavior change, so no new test â€” existing write-path tests in each of the three files already pin the observable behavior. scoped at 35cd3fd.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binaryâ†”npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) â€” no signing creds â€” but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at ac40c72, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff a23269d..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.

summary: 0045 also frees guidance onto a field declaration, no predicate â€” deferred: none of the six promoted built-in notes targets a single field, so there is no consumer yet

gate.reason: 0045's Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level, but the SDK's only per-field metadata today is a clause's `field:` target (predicate-bound) or `EdgeField` (edge-typed only) â€” no 'field declaration' list a bare advisory could attach to. None of the six promoted notes (GUIDANCE-BUILTIN-NOTES-PROMOTE) target a single field, inventing the mechanism now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it ('An export earns its consumer', engineering.md). Revive when a field wants counsel no clause carries.

edit.description: Anticipated: a field-name-keyed guidance fact on `KindFacts`, analogous to `EdgeField`'s field-name-keyed list (196) but for arbitrary fields â€” shape unset; no field in any built-in kind wants one yet.
Anticipated: a `KindFactRow` column (or sibling row) carrying per-field guidance/cite, following the `to_table`/`from_table` pattern GUIDANCE-KIND-SDK-LOCK-FACT establishes for the kind-level pair.

Anticipated: `emit()`'s per-property `description` (156-172) folds field-declaration guidance in alongside a clause's, the same 'join into one description' discipline `push_description` (316-329) already runs for several guided clauses on one field.
Anticipated: field-level narration extends `narrate_governing_contract` (374-402) or a sibling to print a field's declared guidance.

acceptance: A field declaring `guidance` (no predicate) surfaces it via `schema` hover and `explain`, never gating â€” provable once a real kind needs one.
notes: 0045 Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level; this waits for a driver, the same 'no consumer' bar prior deferred entries in this queue's history have held to. scoped at 946e303.
